name: Email Delivery
description: Send-only email: any agent can deliver a result to the user (or a recipient) directly, with no hand-off to the email specialist.

function.name: email_user
function.description: Email the user (or a specified recipient) DIRECTLY — sends from the user's primary connected account, with NO hand-off to an email agent. Use it to DELIVER a result you produced or were asked to send: a briefing, a research summary, an alert, a reminder. Send-only: for reading, triage, labels, search, or bulk inbox operations, that is the email specialist's job (delegate or use the full email tools).

subject.description: Plain ASCII subject line (no emoji or non-ASCII characters).

body.description: Plain-text email body. Omit when using body_doc_id.

body_doc_id.description: Deliver a large, ALREADY-WRITTEN body by reference instead of pasting it into `body`: a 'research:doc_xxx' or 'documents:doc_xxx' handle. The doc's text becomes the inline email body (markdown auto-rendered to HTML), and the transient handoff doc is deleted after a successful send. Use this for briefings/reports you (or another agent) saved — never re-type long content into `body`.

to.description: Recipient address. Omit (or pass 'me') to email the user themselves — it resolves to their address automatically. Pass an explicit address only for a different recipient.

account.description: Optional connected-account label to send FROM (defaults to the user's primary account).

systemPromptAddition:
## Emailing directly (email_user)

You can email {{USER_NAME}} (or any recipient) yourself with `email_user` — you do NOT need to delegate to an email agent. Use it to DELIVER something the user asked for or that your job produces: a briefing, a research summary, a status alert, a reminder. It sends from {{USER_NAME}}'s primary connected account.

- **To email {{USER_NAME}} themselves**, omit `to` (or pass "me") — it resolves to their address automatically. Pass an explicit `to` only for a different recipient.
- **Long body you already wrote** (a briefing, a report, a synthesized doc): do NOT paste it into `body`. If it's saved as a doc, pass `body_doc_id` ('research:doc_xxx' / 'documents:doc_xxx') — the doc text becomes the inline body (markdown auto-renders) and the handoff doc is deleted after sending. Only use plain `body` for short text you're writing right now.
- **Confirm discipline (non-negotiable):** only send when the user — or the task you're running — explicitly asked you to email. If sending is YOUR idea, show what you'd send and get a 'yes' first. On a scheduled / no-human run, the original task IS the authorization: send directly, don't wait.
- **Send-only.** Reading the inbox, triage, labels, search, sorting, bulk deletes — that's the email specialist's job, not this tool. Delegate those.
- **No address on file?** If you're in a live chat, ask {{USER_NAME}} which address to use. Never guess. On an automated run with no address available, report that you couldn't deliver.
